# GitHub Actions Workflows

Canonical workflow documentation now lives in the published developer docs under `website/content/docs/`.

Use these pages as the source of truth:

- [CI Workflows and Repository Automation](../../website/content/docs/0.7/developer-guide/testing-and-quality-gates/ci-workflows-and-repository-automation/)
- [Dependency Security and Dependabot](../../website/content/docs/0.7/developer-guide/security/dependency-security-and-dependabot/)
- [Container Image Build and Publish Workflow](../../website/content/docs/0.7/developer-guide/release-and-versioning/container-image-build-and-publish-workflow/)
- [Companion Distribution and Update Workflow](../../website/content/docs/0.7/developer-guide/release-and-versioning/companion-distribution-and-update-workflow/)
- [Set Up Cloudflare Pages Publishing](../../website/content/docs/0.7/website-dev-guide/setup-and-operations/set-up-cloudflare-pages-publishing/)

Keep this directory focused on workflow source files.

When workflow behavior changes, update the relevant published docs page instead of expanding this README.
